---
title: UIAO Self-Hosted Git Server (Windows Server 2025 + IIS) — Health & Evidence
  — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: uiao-git-server
adapter-class: conformance
mission-class: telemetry
status: active
phase: phase-1
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-12'
---

# UIAO Self-Hosted Git Server (Windows Server 2025 + IIS) — Health & Evidence — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `active` · **Class:** `conformance` · **Mission:** `telemetry` · **Phase:** `phase-1`

**Canon source:** `canon/adapter-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The UIAO Self-Hosted Git Server Adapter is the **substrate-internal infrastructure observer** for the UIAO self-hosted Git service (Windows Server 2025 + IIS). As a conformance-class telemetry adapter, it observes service health, TLS posture, repository inventory, and IIS access audit for the Git infrastructure that hosts the substrate's canon and the customer's governance repositories.

This adapter is one of the few that observes UIAO's own substrate rather than a third-party system. The Git server is foundational — it hosts the canon, the adapter registries, the runbooks, and the OSCAL artifacts — so its operational evidence has to flow into the same continuous-monitoring pack as every other adapter's output. Without this adapter, the substrate's own Git infrastructure is the one component without canonical observability.

Four primary functions:

1. **Service-health observation.** Watches IIS application-pool state, `git http-backend` availability, and Windows service status for the Git endpoints. Emits canonical health claims per polling interval with explicit up / down / degraded classification.
2. **TLS-posture observation.** Inventories the TLS certificates on the Git endpoints — expiry date, chain validity, key algorithm, SAN coverage — emitting canonical claims that drive near-expiry alerts and chain-validation failures.
3. **Repository inventory.** Enumerates the hosted repositories with metadata (size, last-push timestamp, branch count, quota state). The inventory feeds CM-8 component-inventory evidence for the substrate's own hosting plane.
4. **IIS access-audit summary.** Reads the IIS request logs, normalizing into per-period summary claims (request volume, response-code distribution, top-N principal access patterns) for the AU-2 / AU-6 evidence stream.

**Implementation:** Reserved-then-active per ADR-041 (moved from draft to accepted when the implementation guides landed under `docs/docs/ops/git-server/`). Runs on `on-prem-self-hosted` because the adapter requires LAN-side access to the IIS management surface.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `git-service-health`, `git-tls-posture`, `git-repository-inventory`, `git-access-audit`

**Reads:** IIS Web Administration API for application-pool and site state, Windows Service Control Manager for service status, the TLS certificate store on the Git endpoints, the Git repository directory listing, and the IIS request logs (`%SystemDrive%\inetpub\logs\LogFiles\`). Read-only Windows administrative authentication per the documented least-privilege role.

**Emits:** `git-server-health.json` (per-poll service-health record), `git-tls-inventory.json` (per-poll TLS certificate inventory with expiry timestamps), `git-repo-inventory.json` (per-poll repository inventory with size and last-push), plus `EvidenceObject` records that feed the CA-7 / CM-8 / AU-2 / AU-6 / SI-4 control evidence stream.

**Does NOT:** modernize, provision, or modify the Git service (provisioning is handled via IaC — Terraform / DSC — outside the adapter fabric), modify IIS configuration, manage TLS certificate issuance, write to repositories, or modify Windows service configuration.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `CA-7`, `CM-8`, `AU-2`, `AU-6`, `SI-4`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **CA-7** Continuous Monitoring | Primary | Per-poll service-health and TLS-posture observation produces continuous evidence of the Git infrastructure's operational state. |
| **CM-8** Information System Component Inventory | Primary | Repository inventory plus TLS-certificate inventory is the canonical inventory for the substrate's own Git hosting plane. |
| **AU-2** Event Logging | Supporting | IIS access-audit summaries verify that the documented audit categories are being captured by the Git endpoints. |
| **AU-6** Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Supporting | Per-period access-audit summaries support the audit-review process for the Git surface. |
| **SI-4** System Monitoring | Primary | Health, TLS, and access-pattern observation collectively are the system-monitoring evidence for the Git infrastructure. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `python-3.12` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `on-prem-self-hosted` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `interval` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Conformance adapter for the UIAO self-hosted Git service (Windows Server 2025 + IIS). Promoted to active when ADR-041 moved from draft to accepted and the implementation guides landed under `docs/docs/ops/git-server/`. Observes service health (IIS app-pool state, `git http-backend` availability), TLS posture (certificate expiry, chain validity), repository inventory (count + quota), and access audit (IIS request log summary). Pair document: ADR-041. **Does NOT** modernize the Git service itself; provisioning is handled via IaC (Terraform / DSC) outside the adapter fabric.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-041 — UIAO Git infrastructure

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
